initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Every cell of the output grid must be defined
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Number of nonzero input cells
n_nz(N) :- N = #count{ R,C,Color : input(R,C,Color), Color != 0 }.

% Guess the two nonzero cells.
% R1,C1,V1  the first (lexicographically smallest) nonzero cell
% R2,C2,V2  the second nonzero cell
1 { R1,C1,V1,R2,C2,V2 :
    input(R1,C1,V1), input(R2,C2,V2),
    V1 != 0, V2 != 0,
    (R2,C2,V2) != (R1,C1,V1) } 1 :- n_nz = 2.
% Force a deterministic ordering (lexicographic) so that the
% first cell is the one that is smallest in rowcolumn order.
:- (R1*100 + C1) < (R2*100 + C2).

% Residue variables for the periodic pattern (mod6)
1 { col_res1 : (C1 \ 6) = col_res1 } 1 :- input(R1,C1,V1).
1 { col_res2 : (C2 \ 6) = col_res2 } 1 :- input(R2,C2,V2).
1 { row_res1 : (R1 \ 6) = row_res1 } 1 :- input(R1,C1,V1).
1 { row_res2 : (R2 \ 6) = row_res2 } 1 :- input(R2,C2,V2).

% Choose the dimension of the pattern:
%    if the two column residues differ  column pattern (pattern = 0)
%    otherwise (they are equal)  row pattern (pattern = 1)
1 { pattern : 0 } 1 :- col_res1 != col_res2.
1 { pattern : 1 } 1 :- col_res1 = col_res2.

% Choose a colour for each cell (0, V1 or V2)
1 { output(R,C,Out) : Out = 0, Out = V1, Out = V2 } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the periodic pattern
% V1 can appear only where its residue matches the current pattern
:- output(R,C,V1), pattern = 0, (C \ 6) != col_res1.
:- output(R,C,V1), pattern = 1, (R \ 6) != row_res1.
% V2 can appear only where its residue matches the current pattern
:- output(R,C,V2), pattern = 0, (C \ 6) != col_res2.
:- output(R,C,V2), pattern = 1, (R \ 6) != row_res2.
% Zero is forbidden wherever the appropriate residue would put a colour
:- output(R,C,0), pattern = 0, (C \ 6) = col_res1.
:- output(R,C,0), pattern = 0, (C \ 6) = col_res2.
:- output(R,C,0), pattern = 1, (R \ 6) = row_res1.
:- output(R,C,0), pattern = 1, (R \ 6) = row_res2.

#show output/3.

% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Every cell of the output grid must be defined
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Number of nonzero input cells
n_nz(N) :- N = #count{ R,C,Color : input(R,C,Color), Color != 0 }.

% Guess the two nonzero cells.
% R1,C1,V1  the first (lexicographically smallest) nonzero cell
% R2,C2,V2  the second nonzero cell
1 { R1,C1,V1,R2,C2,V2 :
    input(R1,C1,V1), input(R2,C2,V2),
    V1 != 0, V2 != 0,
    (R2,C2,V2) != (R1,C1,V1) } 1 :- n_nz = 2.
% Force a deterministic ordering (lexicographic) so that the
% first cell is the one that is smallest in rowcolumn order.
:- (R1*100 + C1) < (R2*100 + C2).

% Residue variables for the periodic pattern (mod6)
1 { col_res1 : (C1 \ 6) = col_res1 } 1 :- input(R1,C1,V1).
1 { col_res2 : (C2 \ 6) = col_res2 } 1 :- input(R2,C2,V2).
1 { row_res1 : (R1 \ 6) = row_res1 } 1 :- input(R1,C1,V1).
1 { row_res2 : (R2 \ 6) = row_res2 } 1 :- input(R2,C2,V2).

% Choose the dimension of the pattern:
%    if the two column residues differ  column pattern (pattern = 0)
%    otherwise (they are equal)  row pattern (pattern = 1)
1 { pattern : 0 } 1 :- col_res1 != col_res2.
1 { pattern : 1 } 1 :- col_res1 = col_res2.

% Choose a colour for each cell (0, V1 or V2)
1 { output(R,C,Out) : Out = 0, Out = V1, Out = V2 } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% Enforce the periodic pattern
% V1 can appear only where its residue matches the current pattern
:- output(R,C,V1), pattern = 0, (C \ 6) != col_res1.
:- output(R,C,V1), pattern = 1, (R \ 6) != row_res1.
% V2 can appear only where its residue matches the current pattern
:- output(R,C,V2), pattern = 0, (C \ 6) != col_res2.
:- output(R,C,V2), pattern = 1, (R \ 6) != row_res2.
% Zero is forbidden wherever the appropriate residue would put a colour
:- output(R,C,0), pattern = 0, (C \ 6) = col_res1.
:- output(R,C,0), pattern = 0, (C \ 6) = col_res2.
:- output(R,C,0), pattern = 1, (R \ 6) = row_res1.
:- output(R,C,0), pattern = 1, (R \ 6) = row_res2.

#show output/3.
